titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale New Haven Educational Corporation Records
Yale New Haven Educational Corporation Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Cheryl Bliss

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday September 4, 2020 at 07:29

did.unittitle: Yale New Haven Educational Corporation records
did.abstract: The records consist of correspondence, applications for licensing and funding, agreements, and meeting minutes documenting the Yale New Haven Educational Corporation (YNHEC) and its unsuccessful attempt to establish an education television station in New Haven, Connecticut.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 4-C (YRG 4-C), records documenting public information functions of Yale University.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred from the Office of Public Affairs, 1980.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale New Haven Educational Corporation Records (RU 949). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged alphabetically by subject.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Yale New Haven Educational Corporation (YNHEC) was established at Yale in 1967. The Board of Directors included as president, Howard Weaver (associate secretary of Yale University), treasurer, John Ecklund (treasurer of Yale University), and secretary, David Walker (editor of Yale Reports) as well as prominent individuals in the New Haven community. Its purpose was to design, produce, and distribute programs to benefit Yale University and the general public. Additionally, the corporation wanted to build and operate an educational television station in New Haven, Connecticut. Unable to secure funding for the project, the YNHEC dissolved in 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of correspondence, applications for licensing and funding, agreements, and meeting minutes documenting the Yale New Haven Educational Corporation (YNHEC) and its unsuccessful attempt to establish an education television station in New Haven, Connecticut.

corpname.part: Yale University. Office of Public Affairs
